title: rubus hirtus var. aculeatipes
intro: Rubus hirtus var. aculeatipes, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan black raspberry, is a thorny shrub belonging to the Rubus genus, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. aculeatipes include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities. Rubus hirtus var. aculeatipes can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and is also used as a culinary ingredient in traditional Asian and Himalayan cuisine.
